skill.name: facs-gating-viz-style
skill.description: Generate publication-ready flow cytometry gating plots with structured execution, explicit assumptions, and clear output boundaries.
skill.summary: Beautify FACS gating plots for publication with contour, density, and dot plot styles.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 42 static analysis findings across 3 files (527 lines). All findings are false positives: 26 backtick patterns are markdown code fence examples demonstrating legitimate Python script usage; 2 hardcoded URLs are GitHub source attribution links; 2 path traversal references appear in the Security Checklist documenting items to validate against, not actual traversal code; 6 weak cryptographic algorithm detections are parser errors on YAML front matter, documentation text, and argparse usage; 5 system reconnaissance detections are standard parameter descriptions and test case documentation. The skill is a legitimate FACS data visualization tool with no malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Shell Command References in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 26 backtick patterns as Ruby/shell execution. These are markdown code fence blocks in SKILL.md demonstrating legitimate python script usage. No actual command injection or external execution risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged a hardcoded URL as a network risk. This is a GitHub repository link used for source attribution. No network exfiltration or unauthorized API calls present.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Reference in Security Checklist
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged '../' patterns as path traversal sequences. These appear in the Security Checklist section documenting items to validate, not actual directory traversal code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - False Detection
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm at 6 locations. All are false positives: YAML front matter description, script path reference, argparse usage, table formatting, and JSON audit file content. No cryptographic operations are present in this skill.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance - False Detection
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged documentation text as system reconnaissance. The flagged lines contain standard parameter descriptions, audit-ready command documentation, and test case descriptions. No actual reconnaissance logic present.

content.value_statement: Researchers spend hours manually formatting flow cytometry plots for publication. This skill automates FACS gating visualization with publication-ready styling for contour, density, and dot plots.
actual_capabilities: Generate contour plots from FCS flow cytometry data files
Create density visualization plots for cell population analysis
Produce dot plot representations of flow cytometry data
Apply publication-ready styling to all generated plots
Validate input file paths before processing
Provide structured output with documented assumptions and risks
limitations: Does not perform statistical analysis on gating populations
Only supports three plot styles: contour, density, and dot
Requires pre-gated FCS files as input data
Processes one FCS file at a time, no native batch mode

use_cases.title: Publication Figure Generation
use_cases.description: A flow cytometry researcher needs to generate publication-quality contour plots from gated FCS data files for journal submission.

use_cases.title: Standardized Lab Reporting
use_cases.description: A lab manager wants to standardize visualization output across multiple experiments for consistent internal reporting and data review.

use_cases.title: Thesis Figure Preparation
use_cases.description: A graduate student preparing figures for a thesis defense needs properly formatted cytometry plots with clean axis labels and consistent styling.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Contour Plot
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a contour plot from my FCS data at [file_path] using the facs-gating-viz-style skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user generating a simple contour plot from a single FCS file

prompt_templates.title: Density Visualization
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a density plot from my flow cytometry data at [file_path] with publication-ready styling using the facs-gating-viz-style skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher needs density-based cell population visualization

prompt_templates.title: Batch Processing Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Process multiple FCS files in the directory [directory_path] and generate contour plots for each one using the facs-gating-viz-style skill. Validate each input file before processing.
prompt_templates.scenario: Processing multiple samples from a single experiment

prompt_templates.title: Full Publication Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the facs-gating-viz-style workflow to my FCS data at [file_path] with style [contour|density|dot]. Validate inputs, generate the plot, document all assumptions, and provide a structured output report with risks and next steps.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete workflow execution with input validation and structured reporting

output_examples.input: FCS file with single-cell flow cytometry data, requesting contour plot style
output_examples.output: Publication-ready contour plot showing cell population distributions with clean axis labels, appropriate color scheme, and resolution suitable for journal submission.

output_examples.input: Batch of FCS files from a time-course experiment, requesting density plot style for all samples
output_examples.output: Series of density visualization plots with consistent styling across all time points, each with labeled axes and population annotations suitable for comparison.

best_practices: Always run python -m py_compile scripts/main.py to verify the script parses correctly before execution.
Document all assumptions about the input data, including gating strategy and compensation settings.
Use the structured response template to separate assumptions, deliverables, and risks in the output.
anti_patterns: Executing the script without validating the FCS file path first.
Performing statistical analysis on gating populations outside the skill documented scope.
Modifying the source FCS data files during the visualization process.

faq.question: What FCS file formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill reads standard FCS files. Compatibility depends on the Python environment and available flow cytometry libraries.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without Python installed?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires Python 3.10 or higher to execute the packaged script.

faq.question: What plot styles are available?
faq.answer: Three styles are supported: contour, density, and dot. Contour is the default style.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my original FCS data files?
faq.answer: No. The skill reads FCS files as input and generates plot images as output. Original data files are not modified.

faq.question: Can this skill process multiple FCS files in one run?
faq.answer: The current implementation processes one file at a time. For batch processing, run the skill multiple times.

faq.question: What is the output format for generated plots?
faq.answer: Plot output is generated by the Python script. Verify the specific output format by running scripts/main.py --help.
